text: I feel a sense of calm detachment in the first space, as if it is intentionally designed for quiet contemplation or passive waiting. The simplicity and openness evoke a kind of philosophical void: a place for thought, but not action. In the second scene, surrounded by the colorful display of R+D, I feel my mind stirred by possibility and curiosity. Here, research and inquiry are explicitly celebrated, and I sense an invitation to be inquisitive—to actively engage with new ideas. There is an underlying hum of intellectual excitement, contrasting the solemn tranquility of the previous room.
